core_mechanism: a diagrammatic (graphical) calculus in which elements of an algebra are encoded as topological diagrams whose allowed deformations enforce the algebra's relations, so that structural properties such as commutativity and centrality are established by geometric manipulation rather than symbolic computation
brief_reason: The abstract represents the operators as tangles and exhibits a single tangle for their sum that is 'obviously central', i.e. an algebraic property (centrality) is read directly off the diagram's topology rather than computed algebraically.
